channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-20112] sanity test_101k: verify drop_caches evicts non-pinned Lustre client pages
item.description:
Add a sanity test (test_101k) that verifies drop_caches actually evicts Lustre client page cache pages.

Original investigation reframed: Lustre's drop_caches behavior is correct as designed. The kernel-level reclaim path (mapping_evict_folio in mm/truncate.c) checks the folio refcount against (nr_pages + has_private + 1) before calling release_folio. With Lustre's normal page state, this means a folio in the page cache with no in-flight RPC has refcount 3 (page cache + vvp_page_init's get_page + find_lock_entries elevation), which passes the threshold and gets evicted via ll_release_folio -> do_release_page.

What was confusing: write pages stay pinned for replay until the OST commits the write, because osc_brw_prep_request uses ptlrpc_bulk_kiov_pin_ops, which holds an extra get_page on every page in the bulk descriptor until ptlrpc_release_bulk_page_pin runs. This is intentional and required for write replay during recovery – the window between completion and commit can be arbitrarily long.

That means:

  • Pages from a read-only or already-committed file: drop_caches works.
  • Pages from a freshly-written file (commit not yet received): drop_caches DOES NOT evict them, by design.

There is no bug. test_101k validates the working behavior:
1. Write 4 MiB + conv=fsync (forces server commit -> bulk pin released)
2. drop_caches=1
3. Re-read and confirm at least one ost_read happened (cache miss)
4. Same flow without writing: cancel_lru_locks osc, read, drop, re-read, confirm cache miss

Side note (worth a separate ticket if anyone cares): global sync(2) does NOT drain Lustre's bulk pins. Only fsync(fd) (or per-file conv=fsync) does, because only fsync goes through ll_fsync -> OST_SYNC. Making sync(2) wait for OST commit would be expensive on every call, so leaving it as-is.

The original symptom report was investigated under https://review.whamcloud.com/65222 (now abandoned – the proposed fix was a no-op in dead code, see review for details).
item.summary: sanity test_101k: verify drop_caches evicts non-pinned Lustre client pages

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65227
Subject: LU-20112 tests: verify drop_caches evicts client cache pages
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 63d458577a785ddc2b7e2fcc158584fde1552515

Reframing this ticket. Original investigation reported drop_caches failing on Lustre, but a closer look (with mincore() and bpftrace) shows drop_caches works correctly – pages from a read-only or already-committed file are evicted as expected. The cases that 'failed' were pages still bulk-pinned for write replay (osc_brw_prep_request uses ptlrpc_bulk_kiov_pin_ops, which holds an extra get_page until the OST commit reaps the request). That is intentional and required for recovery.

Specifically, the kernel's mapping_evict_folio refcount check (>nr_pages+has_private+1, i.e. >3) passes for ordinary cached pages and rejects pages still bulk-pinned. With the bulk pin held the refcount is 4, without it 3. So the 'fix' in https://review.whamcloud.com/65222 was solving a non-problem – the put_page it added in do_release_page was in dead code, because release_folio is never called when the bulk pin is still held.

Action: abandon https://review.whamcloud.com/65222 (done) and add sanity test_101k that verifies drop_caches actually evicts both read pages and write+fsync pages.

Side note: global sync(2) does NOT drain Lustre's bulk pins. Only fsync(fd) goes through ll_fsync -> OST_SYNC and forces commit. Documenting in case it surprises anyone else; not fixing because making sync(2) wait for OST commit would be expensive on every call.

"Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/65222
Subject: LU-20112 llite: drop vmpage ref in do_release_page
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 2c5c6c6dc00c221ca9d4cc75e5dfffbcffce864f
